title: validate-block-items gains disclosure AND an installed-basis variant -- both halves of the surface fix, default unchanged
context: FGAP-145: validate-block-items resolves against the catalog schema plus a self-seeded catalog-chain registry, returning valid:true on blocks every canonical read throws on (false-green reproduced out-of-the-box on a fresh install). Its filed proposed_resolution assigns the mechanism determination -- (a) re-surface the op to disclose its catalog-forward-preview semantics versus (b) provide a read-path-faithful diagnostic -- to the implementing pass. The choice is determinable from the system's own facts: (i) the two halves are complementary, not exclusive -- disclosure fixes the mis-consumption of the existing purpose, faithfulness supplies the missing question's answer; the gap's own class statement (a validity op whose surfaced name does not disclose its resolution) requires disclosure regardless of which validation basis exists; (ii) the precedent instance of this class was closed by aligning the divergent surface to the read path's resolution (context-validate's sweep uses validateBlockWithMigrationForDir), so the faithful basis reuses that exact resolution; (iii) the op surface's established convention prefers a parameter on the same op over a sibling op when the question is identical with a different axis (the projection and pagination parameters follow this shape), so the faithful variant is a basis parameter, not a new op; (iv) callers exist for the current preview semantics (the update-preview purpose is documented and real), so the DEFAULT must remain the catalog-forward preview -- a default flip would silently change every existing caller's question.
decision: validate-block-items (1) always returns a resolution field in its result envelope naming the basis it validated against (catalog-forward-preview by default), with its description and promptSnippet stating the catalog + self-seeded-registry semantics explicitly; and (2) gains an optional basis parameter -- catalog (default, current behavior byte-unchanged) or installed -- where installed validates against the installed schema plus the project migration registry via the same validateBlockWithMigrationForDir resolution the canonical read path uses, non-throwing: it returns valid:false with the failure detail for a block the read path would throw on. The default stays catalog.
consequences: An operator asking 'does this block read?' gets a truthful answer from basis=installed -- the fresh-install false-green reproduction flips (validate-block-items reports the unreadable block invalid where read-block throws), while the catalog-forward update-preview purpose remains available and byte-unchanged under the default.
The verdict is un-mistakable for a canonical-read verdict either way: the envelope's resolution field discloses the basis on every call, and the op strings state both semantics.
The faithful basis reuses the read path's exact resolution helper, so the diagnostic and the read gate cannot disagree -- the identical-verdict property the class precedent established.

Derivation basis (decision_derived_from_item -> FGAP-114): the prior instance of this disclosure/faithfulness class was closed by aligning the divergent surface to the read-path resolution; this decision applies the same alignment as an opt-in basis while disclosure closes the mis-consumption the class names. The fork is resolved, not escalated.

references.label: The gap's investigation: the false-green reproduction, the surface-by-surface class sweep, the both-halves framing
The read-path resolution the installed basis reuses
